answer:
**Chapter 11 of Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara Part 1* is titled "Sakhtiyan karta hoon dil par, ghair se ghafil hoon main"** (the opening line of the poem in the original Urdu).[^1]

This is a ghazal (or nazm in verse form), and its **matlaʿ** (opening couplet/sher) opens the first paragraph in translation as: "...I am harsh upon my heart; of all else, heedless I am. / ...ah, how well it is said: a tyrant I am, an ignorant one I am." The claim is the speaker's self-description as rigorously self-disciplined yet oblivious and tyrannical toward his own heart—emphasizing spiritual austerity, ignorance of the external world ("ghair," or others/strangers), and ultimate self-absorption.[^2]

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 11, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/11/translation/).

[^2]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 11, [p-941acb](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/11/translation/#p-941acb).
